text: When you write code, you will sometimes find errors, or "bugs." Identifying bugs is the first step in fixing them. A systematic approach is helpful: start by checking the most recent changes you made, then test each part of your code separately. Many debugging tools can make this process easier. For example, most code editors have a debugger that lets you pause your program and inspect variables. Another useful tool is logging. By adding log statements to your code, you can track its flow and see where things go wrong. Using these strategies will help you solve problems more quickly and confidently.
